sanskrit:
त्रिविधा भवति श्रद्धा देहिनां सा स्वभावजा।
सात्त्विकी राजसी चैव तामसी चेति तां श्रृणु॥
transliteration:
tri-vidhā bhavati śraddhā dehināṃ sā svabhāva-jā |
sāttvikī rājasī caiva tāmasī ceti tāṃ śṛṇu ||
literal_meaning: Three kinds of faith arise in embodied beings, born of their own nature—goodness, passion, and ignorance—hear about them.
contextual_meaning: Krishna tells Arjuna that every person’s core convictions (śraddhā) are not chosen but conditioned by the psychological mode (guṇa) that currently dominates the mind; these modes are malleable through conscious association and practice.
psychological_state: Arjuna is open but unsettled, realizing that his own beliefs may be products of unconscious patterns rather than free choices.
trigger_scenario: A person scrolling social media notices they instantly trust posts that match their political tribe and dismiss the rest—recognizing their ‘faith’ is algorithmically and temperamentally conditioned.
anchor_text: Notice which inner voice—calm, restless, or dull—shapes your beliefs today.
